full_name: Ministry of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship
blurb: Stewards B.C.'s water, land and natural resources — water licensing and dam safety, Crown land tenures and land use planning, and fish and wildlife management including hunting and freshwater fishing.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbians have secure, sustainably managed water
goals.description: The ministry licenses the use of surface water and groundwater, regulates dam safety, and helps communities and water users prepare for drought and flooding.

main_services.name: Get a licence to use water

supporting.name: Apply for a water licence
supporting.desc: A water right authorizing the diversion and use of surface water or non-domestic groundwater is granted through FrontCounter BC and reviewed by a water manager under the Water Sustainability Act.

legislation.full_name: Water Sustainability Act  -  Water licences and approvals

supporting.name: Apply for a short-term water use approval
supporting.desc: A time-limited approval under the Water Sustainability Act authorizes short-term diversion or use of water, such as for construction dewatering or temporary irrigation.

channels.label: Apply for a water use approval - short term

legislation.full_name: Water Sustainability Act  -  Water licences and approvals

supporting.name: Apply for a change approval or notify the ministry of in-stream work
supporting.desc: Making changes in and about a stream — such as building a crossing, bank protection, or other works near water — requires a licence, change approval, or notification under the Water Sustainability Act.
supporting.users: Contractors, landowners, businesses working near streams

channels.label: Apply for a Change Approval or Submit Notification of Instream Work

legislation.full_name: Water Sustainability Act  -  Working around water

supporting.name: Register your domestic water well
supporting.desc: Well owners can register wells used for household or domestic livestock needs, which do not require a water licence, in the provincial Groundwater Wells and Aquifers (GWELLS) database.

legislation.full_name: Water Sustainability Act  -  Groundwater Protection Regulation

supporting.name: Get a groundwater licence for non-domestic use
supporting.desc: Anyone using groundwater for irrigation, commercial or industrial purposes must hold a water licence under the Water Sustainability Act and pay applicable fees and rentals.

legislation.full_name: Water Sustainability Act  -  Non-domestic groundwater use

supporting.name: Search water rights and licence records
supporting.desc: The Water Rights Databases let landowners, licensees and the public search existing water licences and approvals on a stream or aquifer.

main_services.name: Manage drought, flood and dam risk

supporting.name: Check drought conditions and water restrictions
supporting.desc: The B.C. Drought Information Portal tracks drought levels across the province and shares water conservation guidance for households, farms and communities facing water shortages.

supporting.name: Get river flow forecasts and flood warnings
supporting.desc: The River Forecast Centre analyses snowpack and issues seasonal water supply, freshet and flood risk forecasts and warnings for B.C.'s rivers and streams.
supporting.users: General public, local governments, emergency managers

supporting.name: Meet dam safety inspection and reporting requirements
supporting.desc: Owners of the province's 1,900+ regulated dams must meet construction, inspection, emergency planning and reporting requirements under the Water Sustainability Act and Dam Safety Regulation.
supporting.users: Dam owners, hydropower operators, agricultural dam owners

channels.label: Contact the B.C. Dam Safety Program

legislation.full_name: Water Sustainability Act  -  Dam Safety Regulation

supporting.name: Join or manage a Water Users' Community
supporting.desc: Groups of six or more water licensees who share a diversion, storage or delivery system can form a Water Users' Community under the Water Users' Communities Act to jointly maintain their works.
supporting.users: Groups of water licensees, irrigation districts

legislation.full_name: Water Users' Communities Act  -  Formation and governance of water users' communities

supporting.name: Get water service from a private water utility
supporting.desc: About 30,000 B.C. households get domestic water from privately owned and operated water utilities, which must provide safe, clean water under the Water Utility Act and Utilities Commission Act.

legislation.full_name: Water Utility Act  -  Regulation of private water utilities

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Provincial public land is used and stewarded sustainably
goals.description: The ministry authorizes the use of Crown land and water, leads strategic land use planning, and provides the geographic data that underpins resource decisions across B.C.

main_services.name: Get authorization to use Crown land

supporting.name: Apply to lease, purchase or use Crown land
supporting.desc: Applications for tenures, leases, licences of occupation and Crown grants over provincial Crown land are submitted through FrontCounter BC, the province's window for natural resource permitting.

legislation.full_name: Land Act  -  Disposition of Crown land

supporting.name: Search the Crown land registry
supporting.desc: The Government Access Tool for Online Retrieval (GATOR) lets applicants and the public search the Crown land registry for existing tenures and land status.

legislation.full_name: Land Act  -  Crown land registry

supporting.name: Get a Crown land tenure for aggregate or quarry material extraction
supporting.desc: Businesses that extract sand, gravel or quarry material from Crown land require a tenure specific to aggregate and quarry material use.

legislation.full_name: Land Act  -  Crown land uses

supporting.name: Get a Crown land tenure for a recreation or tourism business
supporting.desc: Commercial recreation and tourism operators need a tenure to run guided, lodge-based or other tourism activities on provincial Crown land.

legislation.full_name: Land Act  -  Crown land uses

supporting.name: Register an off-road vehicle for use on Crown land
supporting.desc: A cross-government framework requires registration of off-road vehicles used on Crown land, part of measures to manage growing off-road vehicle use and protect people and the environment.

supporting.name: Report misuse of Crown land
supporting.desc: Members of the public can report unauthorized occupation, dumping, or other misuse of provincial Crown land to the ministry.

channels.label: Report a misuse of Crown land

main_services.name: Plan and map provincial public land

supporting.name: Take part in land use planning for public land and water
supporting.desc: Land use planning sets strategic direction for the 94% of B.C. that is provincial public land, guiding sustainable resource stewardship in partnership with First Nations, local governments and communities.
supporting.users: First Nations, local governments, stakeholders, communities

supporting.name: Access Crown land and resource mapping data
supporting.desc: GeoBC provides web-based mapping, imagery, location APIs and topographic data that support land ownership, permitting and resource decisions across the province.

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: B.C.'s fish and wildlife are healthy and sustainably used
goals.description: The ministry licenses hunting, angling and guiding, manages wildlife populations and habitat, and helps protect species and ecosystems at risk.

supporting.name: Buy a hunting licence
supporting.desc: B.C. resident, non-resident and non-resident alien hunting licences are bought through the WILD system or in person, and are linked to a Fish and Wildlife ID (FWID) profile.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Hunting licences

supporting.name: Apply for Limited Entry Hunting
supporting.desc: Limited Entry Hunting (LEH) draws let wildlife managers control hunter numbers for a species, area and time period beyond what general open seasons allow, with random draws run through WILD.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Limited entry hunting regulation

supporting.name: Get a guide outfitter licence
supporting.desc: A guide outfitter licence authorizes guiding resident and non-resident hunters for big game in an exclusive guide area, issued for a 1-year or 5-year term after passing an exam on the Wildlife Act.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Guide outfitter licensing

relationships.service: Report a poacher, polluter or environmental violation (RAPP)

supporting.name: Hire or become an assistant hunting guide
supporting.desc: Guide outfitters can hire licensed assistant hunting guides to help guide clients within their guiding territory.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Guide outfitter licensing

supporting.name: Report your hunting harvest
supporting.desc: Hunters must submit compulsory harvest reports for certain species, and some species require compulsory inspection within a set number of days of the kill.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Compulsory reporting and inspection

supporting.name: Trap fur-bearing animals in B.C.
supporting.desc: Trapping fur-bearing animals in B.C. requires a trapping licence and compliance with Wildlife Act trapping regulations.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Trapping licences

supporting.name: Buy a freshwater fishing licence
supporting.desc: Recreational freshwater fishing licences are one-day, eight-day or annual, purchased through WILD or in person, with fee reductions for seniors and residents with disabilities.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Freshwater fishing licensing

supporting.name: Get an angling guide licence
supporting.desc: Providing guided freshwater fishing services in B.C. requires a valid angling guide licence.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Angling guide licensing

relationships.service: Report a poacher, polluter or environmental violation (RAPP)

supporting.name: Check freshwater fishing regulations before you fish
supporting.desc: The Freshwater Fishing Regulations Synopsis summarizes water- and species-specific rules, bag limits and in-season changes anglers must follow.

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Freshwater fishing regulations

supporting.name: Report human-wildlife conflict
supporting.desc: The public can report conflicts between people and wildlife, such as dangerous encounters or property damage, to help the ministry respond and prevent future conflicts.

relationships.service: Get compensation for crop or livestock losses caused by wildlife

supporting.name: Get involved in wildlife stewardship and habitat conservation
supporting.desc: Together for Wildlife and related stewardship programs let British Columbians take part in conserving wildlife and their habitats across the province.
supporting.users: General public, conservation groups, Indigenous communities

supporting.name: Check if a species or ecosystem is at risk in B.C.
supporting.desc: The BC Species and Ecosystems Explorer and Conservation Data Centre track species and ecosystems assessed as endangered, threatened or of special concern.

supporting.name: Get your harvest tested for Chronic Wasting Disease
supporting.desc: Hunters harvesting deer, elk or moose in designated management units must submit samples for mandatory Chronic Wasting Disease testing at a designated drop-off location within one week of harvest.

channels.label: Chronic Wasting Disease surveillance and testing

legislation.full_name: Wildlife Act  -  Compulsory reporting and inspection

foundational_services.name: Water science, monitoring and groundwater data
foundational_services.desc: Provincial groundwater observation well monitoring, the Water Science Series and other hydrological research support water licensing and drought and flood decisions.

foundational_services.name: Land and resource stewardship policy and planning
foundational_services.desc: Policy, spatial data and legal direction that guide land use plans and resource stewardship decisions across provincial public land.

foundational_services.name: Fish and wildlife population science and data
foundational_services.desc: Hunter harvest data, wildlife health surveillance and population data support wildlife allocation, licensing and conservation decisions.

foundational_services.name: Coordinate First Nations consultation on land and resource decisions
foundational_services.desc: Consultation and negotiation with First Nations informs land use planning, Crown land authorizations and natural resource decision-making across the ministry.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, First Nations governments

cross_cutting: Grow the economy and create jobs
Reduce costs for families, including housing
